what_it_does: This file is a research-first memo that inventories the existing memory and recall substrate, identifies a missing universal memory leg and a one-directional channel seam, and proposes small wiring changes to ground every resolved address and channel exchange in common company memory.
observations: The memory substrate is described as mostly LIVE: corpus spaces (~5,862 vectors), query_corpus, neighbours, rerank, find_relations, and capture_corpus are all operational.
recall_for_decision is labeled BUILT-BUT-ORPHANED: the compute exists but is imported by nothing and its docstring references a nonexistent route.
Conversation memory is split across three parallel stores (history corpus, per-session recall-index sidecars, substrate.db) with three embedding paths, so 'one shared memory' is not yet true.
Live channels (cc_channels.py and session_channels.py) carry traffic but make zero calls to corpus/recall/embed/capture; they neither read memory nor write exchanges into it.
The designed but unbuilt connector is called 'the 5th wire: channel-scoped recall'.
Progress is gated on issue #69, with three partial sub-gaps: COMPANY_SESSION_ID injection, member-session discovery, and live/continuous capture.
resolve_address has 17 schemes with a documented add-a-scheme seam at cognition.py:1029; exchange://, file://, and project:// are already registered and reserved for recollection.
territory_for composes legs (identity, corpus_record, context, relations, library) and is folded into every brain turn via bridge.py _claude_stream.
Two insertion points are proposed: a recall:// scheme at cognition.py:1029, or a memory leg added to territory_for at territory.py:167.

BEYOND extrapolations include universal auto-grounding, channel exchange becoming memory, proactive memory pushed into channels, operator-memory as a per-address leg, and structural memory via a crossings graph.
standouts: Headline finding: the memory substrate is built and fed; the missing piece is one universal memory leg.
recall_for_decision is built but orphaned, so the compute already exists and only needs wiring.
territory_for is already folded into every brain turn, meaning one memory leg would auto-ground every resolved address on the V-surface.
Channels are one-directional and narrow: they carry live traffic but never read from or write to memory.
The BEYOND claim that explanation should resolve through recall by default, not only for decisions.
conventions: Uses explicit state labels: LIVE, BUILT-BUT-ORPHANED, DESIGNED-UNBUILT, SPEC-ONLY.
Cites file:line references to ground claims.
Uses tables to inventory substrate components.
Includes 'ancestral recall' with verbatim quotes from Tim.
concerns: Heavy dependence on specific file:line claims that can drift as code changes.
recall_for_decision's docstring claims a route that does not exist.
The three parallel conversation-memory stores contradict the 'one shared memory' framing.
Channel capture is gated on a self-modification patch that is reportedly auto-denied.
The memo sometimes blends research observation with design advocacy, e.g., 'the build = fork adds the memory leg'.
notes: A research memo arguing that common company memory is already mostly built and only needs one universal leg wired into territory_for or a recall:// resolver to ground every address, plus a channel capture seam gated on #69.
questions: Are the two proposed insertion points (recall:// scheme vs. territory memory leg) alternatives or intended to be complementary?
What is the current status and content of issue #69?
What do the shorthand labels RHM, G8, and GC14 refer to?
How was recall_for_decision originally intended to be invoked if its claimed route does not exist?
What concrete criteria distinguish the 'BEYOND' lens from the core proposal?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is research-first design input for the memory surface; the content matches, though it also reads as a build proposal and advocacy memo.
apparent_themes: One shared substrate versus fragmented session memory
intention_signals: It tries to reframe the memory-surface build from 'create a new memory system' to 'wire one existing orphan compute into the universal address composer,' and to persuade the lead that channels need bidirectional memory read/write to fulfill the ancestral vision of persistent channels.
novelty: The distinctive reframing that memory-under-the-surface is not a large architecture but a single leg in an already-universal composer, plus the extrapolation that every address scheme (run://, board://, code://, ui://, mind://) could auto-ground in recall by default.
contribution: Keeps the inventory of live memory components, the state labels, the file:line evidence, the honest gap about three conversation-memory stores, the two concrete insertion points (recall:// scheme and territory memory leg), and the beyond extrapolations about channel capture and proactive injection.
summary_for_embedding: A research memo inventorying the company's live memory and recall substrate, identifying a built-but-orphaned recall_for_decision and a missing universal memory leg, and proposing small wiring changes to ground every resolved address and channel exchange in common memory.
intention_for_embedding: To provide design input arguing that memory-under-the-surface is mostly built and should be wired into territory_for and channel capture rather than rebuilt from scratch.
